title: althaea borealis
intro: Althaea borealis, commonly known as the marshmallow plant or sea mallows, is a perennial plant native to the Arctic tundra. The plant contains mucilages, a type of soluble fiber, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Althaea borealis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. Herbal preparations of Althaea borealis include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues.
